QF.id: VI Debt Instruments and Government Securities_9

question.text: Can instruments that are listed, unlisted, or privately placed, or even issued to a single holder, be dematerialised?

answer.text: Yes, irrespective of whether they are listed/unlisted/privately placed or issued to a single holder
No, only listed instruments can be dematerialised
Only instruments issued to multiple holders can be dematerialised
Only instruments approved by SEBI can be dematerialised

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Instruments like bonds, debentures, CP, CDs, irrespective whether listed/unlisted/privately placed or even issued to a single holder, can be dematerialised. **The Trap:** One might assume only listed instruments qualify, but the depository system admits all types. **Cross-Exam Context:** Scope of dematerialisation is tested in NISM Series VI. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Even single-holder private placement bonds can be dematerialised — advise all debt holders to opt for demat regardless of listing status.
